faqs.question: What are the most common pantry pests in Boerum Hill homes?
faqs.answer: Indian meal moths, cigarette beetles, sawtoothed grain beetles, and flour beetles are the most common pantry pests we treat in Boerum Hill. These insects infest dry goods like cereals, flour, pasta, pet food, nuts, and dried fruits, often arriving in contaminated packages from stores or spreading from neighboring units in multi-family buildings.

faqs.question: How long does pantry pest treatment take in Boerum Hill?
faqs.answer: Most pantry pest treatments in Boerum Hill take 1-2 hours depending on the size of your kitchen and severity of infestation. Our process includes thorough inspection, removal of infested materials, targeted treatment of cracks and crevices, and prevention recommendations. You can typically use your kitchen the same day after treatment.

faqs.question: What's included in the $350-$550 pantry pest control service?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive service includes a detailed inspection of all food storage areas, identification of pest species, treatment of infested zones with EPA-approved products, elimination of larval and adult pests, sealing of entry points, and a complete prevention plan with storage recommendations. Price varies based on kitchen size, infestation severity, and whether follow-up visits are needed.

faqs.question: How can I prevent pantry pests from returning to my Boerum Hill home?
faqs.answer: Store all dry goods in airtight glass or heavy plastic containers, regularly clean pantry shelves and corners, inspect groceries before storing them, rotate older items to the front, and dispose of expired foods promptly. We also recommend checking package seals at the store and freezing bulk grains for 3-4 days before pantry storage to kill any potential eggs.
